title: Nicole  D'Aoust
content:
People.ContactEmail nkdaoust@mccarthy.ca
- University of Ottawa
Nicole D’Aoust is a trusted advisor to charities, foundations and non-profits seeking practical and insightful guidance across Canada’s complex legal and regulatory framework.
Nicole D’Aoust is a partner in McCarthy Tétrault’s national Charities and Non-Profits practice and is well-equipped to help tax-exempt entities navigate a dynamic and evolving landscape. Her practice encompasses organizational structuring, governance, CRA compliance, charitable receipting, fundraising, endowments, investments, social finance, and cross-border operations.
Clients consistently praise Nicole’s ability to handle sophisticated matters with clarity and precision, and value her judgment and ability to navigate sensitive issues. She brings a unique blend of technical tax expertise and sector-specific insight. Nationally recognized, Nicole is Chambers-ranked in Charities/Non-profits and is widely regarded as a leading lawyer in this space. Her practice is particularly valued by organizations seeking practical, high-quality advice on regulatory and operational matters, including audits, restructurings, mergers, and advocacy-related compliance.
Prior to focusing on charity law, she practised corporate tax, tax litigation, and dispute resolution at a Big Four accounting firm. She has completed CPA Canada’s three-year In-Depth Tax Course and held a leadership role with the Canadian Tax Foundation. A frequent speaker and writer on sector-related issues, Nicole previously served as co-editor of Canadian Tax Focus and has co-chaired the CBA Charity Law Symposium since 2022.
She was a co-presenter at the ABA Business Law Spring Meeting (Non-Profit Organizations Subcommittee) on cross-border charity granting rules in April 2024; presented “Eliminating Charitable Red Tape” at the Canadian Animal Law Conference, hosted by Toronto Metropolitan University, in September 2024; and has co-authored two papers with Robert Hayhoe for the Canadian Tax Foundation.
Nicole is deeply engaged in shaping the legal landscape for charities in Canada. She was appointed to the federal government’s Technical Issues Working Group (TIWG) as a representative of Imagine Canada, advising the CRA on regulatory issues affecting registered charities and qualified donees. She is a member of the Charities and Not-for-Profit Law Executive of the Canadian Bar Association, a member of a governance sub-committee of the Ontario Bar Association executive, and is chair of the Government Relations Committee of the Canadian Association of Gift Planners. Outside the office, she has held volunteer leadership roles with the Toronto Symphony Orchestra.
Known for her collaborative approach, Nicole plays a key role in McCarthy Tétrault’s team-based service model.
Nicole holds a JD from the University of Ottawa. During law school, she interned with the Supreme Court of Canada’s law branch, competed internationally in commercial negotiation, and served as Senior Articles Editor of the Ottawa Law Review . She was recognized by the Faculty of Law for her academic excellence and leadership. She was called to the Bar of Ontario in 2012 and is fluent in French.
